programmes.show_description: This episode focuses on some of the elderly patients who are treated at King's College Hospital, including 83-year-old William, who hasn't seen a doctor for 40 years.
...Penelope Keith: Brand new series - At Mapperton House, the Countess of Sandwich is under the bed. And architect Edwin Lutyens's original designs are restored to Ashby Manor. (Ep9)
A powerful and cautionary episode following the work of King's College Hospital's A&E department on the night when Whitney Houston died.
This episode focuses on some of the elderly patients who are treated at King's College Hospital, including 83-year-old William, who hasn't seen a doctor for 40 years.
Some favourite investigations. Jimmy Doherty visits Spain to ask how much orange is in orange squash, Matt Tebbutt runs across a pond of custard and Kate Quilton lifts the lid on Marmite.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
At Tyn-yr-Onnen Farm near Snowdonia National Park, the campers embrace farm life, from feeding alpacas and herding sheep to picking exotic mushrooms. But is it worth five stars?

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien turn unwanted items into cash for their owners. In Yorkshire, they scour Wensleydale for profitable finds and celebrate Henry's birthday.
Janine and Mark are hoping to find their perfect holiday home in their favourite spot, France's western Dordogne region, on a budget of £230k. Can Laura Hamilton deliver their dream?
South London. The first host, security guard Michelle, serves a 'pineapple paradise' and her first attempt at a vegan meal. Will there be more dystopian dishes than blissful bites? (Ep1/5)
South London. Mike oozes confidence as he treats his guests to Caribbean food in full Elvis get-up. But will there be suspicious minds when he serves up shop-bought ice cream? (Ep2/5)
South London. Staar hopes to chill her guests out with meditation, crystals and a vegan menu. Can her earth mother vibes win them over, or will they remain confirmed carnivores? (Ep3/5)
South London. Super-organised Nesha goes all out with an authentic Jamaican menu and a classy-but-sexy dress code. She's bigged herself up all week but can she deliver on the night? (Ep4/5)
South London. Events manager Rory tops off the week with an Asian-themed menu. Decked out in his finery, he pulls out all the stops, but will a dessert disaster scupper his chances? (Ep5/5)
Karen and her son Liam welcome their rivals to the luxurious Jockhedge Holiday Cottages in Burgh le Marsh, Lincolnshire. But not everything lives up to the high-end price tag...
Carolynne and her son George have high hopes for their brand-new B&B, The Victoria Inn in Witton-le-Wear, County Durham. But one guest gets locked in the loo and breakfast gets a bashing.
Aidan and Mij host at the Earlston House Hotel in Paignton, Devon. One guest gets a bum deal with the loo roll and, at breakfast, there's kitchen confusion and sausage snobbery.
The final visit is to Pete Lacey's Beach House in Portland, Dorset. But his back-to-basics B&B doesn't do it for everyone, and Karen and Liam's feedback really gets Pete's goat.
It's the final day of the competition. After scores are settled on everything from sleepless nights to smelly showers, the payments are revealed and the winner is announced.
Ford Pop Hot Rod: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend fix up Andy's 1950s Ford Pop hot rod, which is jam-packed with modifications - but they're all bent, broken or riddled with rust...
BMW E30 Alpina: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend fix up an iconic BMW E30 Alpina. It's Maz's pride and joy, but his ill health has prevented him from getting it back on the road.
The Bearsly-Young family try to fit husky-cross Elly in. Annika and Evie try to tame stressball Bunji. And pocket-rocket Raff leaps into Dave and Shelley's home.
Zimbabwean-born Davi and Matt from Australia set out to build a house in Home Counties suburbia with a radical multicultural design. Can they successfully balance a wide range of looks?
Devoted dad Danny builds a sustainable but affordable stilted house up among the trees in his garden. But with no self-build experience and spiralling costs, can he do it on ambition alone?
This dramatic and eye-opening episode focuses on a single Saturday night at King's, where the patients include a stabbing victim with the knife still in place.
This episode focuses on mothers and daughters, including Jackie, who's trying to come to terms with her terminally ill mother Josephine's mortality.
Paramedics have to change their plans when a pregnant woman's waters break at home. And a daughter's worried about her mum, who's collapsed and is confused.
This dramatic and eye-opening episode focuses on a single Saturday night at King's, where the patients include a stabbing victim with the knife still in place.
This episode focuses on mothers and daughters, including Jackie, who's trying to come to terms with her terminally ill mother Josephine's mortality.
A selection of favourite investigations. Jimmy Doherty finds out what gives piquanté peppers their sweetness. Kate Quilton investigates potentially dangerous levels of mercury in tuna fish.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
The campers go wild at Mynydd Derwydd near Clocaenog Forest with a bushcraft skills workshop. But with next to no facilities, will 250 acres of stunning scenery win them over?

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien turn unwanted items into cash for their owners. In Kent, the boys are in stitches over some serious sewing machines.
Diane and John have holidayed on Spain's Costa Cálida for many years and now want to buy their very own property there. Can Craig Rowe find their dream holiday home for £180k?
South London. Stylist Felicia treats her guests to a fashion and glam-themed night, complete with red carpet catwalk. But do her guests think she has that je ne sais quoi? (Ep1/5)
South London. Musician Be hopes to impress his guests with a gastronomic world tour and travel-themed game. Will his holiday vibe fly high or will he hit turbulence along the way? (Ep2/5)
South London. TV editor Joe hosts a 90s-themed night. But will his smile fade as his starter scorches, or can he cool the heat in the kitchen with his home-made ice cream? (Ep3/5)
South London. Video director Dean plans to knock the socks off his guests with his tried-and-tested fiery prawn saganaki and espresso martini brownies. But is it enough to win? (Ep4/5)
South London. Yoga teacher Helen brings calm to the end of the week with her Thai-inspired menu and a Zen yoga-themed night. But will simmering tensions ruin the positive vibes? (Ep5/5)
Mark Canny hosts at The Bowling Green in Chorlton, where some of his guests rave about the newly refurbished rooms but others find faults. And a fridge feud leaves Mark far from chilled...
James and Fiona host at their pole-perched premier pods at Redhill Holidays in Whitbourne. Some of their guests are bowled over by the surroundings but others are bugged by creepy-crawlies.
Andrew and Qidi host at the Mode Lytham Hotel in Lancashire, where they hope to bag best in show. But some problematic parking and a 'grab-and-go' breakfast don't do it for everyone.
Petra and Martin welcome their rivals to Villa Farm Cottage in Thornton Curtis, where the bathroom tiles make one guest nostalgic and a generous breakfast leaves everyone spoilt for choice.
It's the final day of the competition. After dramatic discoveries and score-settling on everything from noisy fridges to parking, the payments are revealed and the winner is announced.
Fiesta XR2: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend fix up a partially restored 1980s Ford Fiesta XR2 for Brian, who's been experiencing health problems.
Honda CR-X: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend fix up their first Honda: a 32-year-old CR-X pocket rocket that's small in size but big on fun. Or this one was - until mice got at it.
Sweetie the Staffy tries to hit Hillary and Jaxson's sweet spot, and senior snaggletooth George looks for the perfect retirement home with expat Janice.
Graeme and Melanie want their new home in Hackney to celebrate their blended family. But squeezing the bright red-clad house onto their tiny garage plot proves a huge challenge.
Brand new series: Can Margot's 700-piece Barbie collection keep her home on the water afloat? Ben's hoping to turn a vintage audio kit into his lifelong dream of a Spitfire flight. (Ep5/6)
Brand new series: Paramedics treat a man having a heart attack who thinks he's just pulled a muscle. And a day out hiking ends badly for a man who's fallen and injured his ankle.
